name: Pyomo (Python Optimization Modeling)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3); Sandia National Laboratories / COIN-OR

auth.notes: Local library; requires external solver (GLPK, Gurobi, CPLEX, CBC, IPOPT)

performance.notes: Performance depends entirely on solver choice; Pyomo itself is problem formulation layer

agent_readiness.notes: Algebraic modeling language for linear/nonlinear/integer programming. from pyomo.environ import *. model = ConcreteModel(). model.x = Var(domain=NonNegativeReals). model.obj = Objective(expr=model.x + 2*model.y, sense=minimize). model.con = Constraint(expr=model.x + model.y >= 1). solver = SolverFactory('glpk'). results = solver.solve(model). Solver-agnostic: GLPK (free), CBC (free), Gurobi (commercial), CPLEX (commercial), IPOPT (nonlinear). Abstract and Concrete models. Stochastic programming. MPEC. More complex than PuLP but more powerful.

gotchas: Solver installation is separate from Pyomo (GLPK, CBC must be installed)
More verbose than PuLP for simple LP/MIP problems
Steep learning curve for abstract model patterns
Commercial solvers (Gurobi, CPLEX) required for best performance on large problems
best_when: Complex optimization: nonlinear, stochastic, large-scale LP/MIP
When OR-Tools or PuLP lack needed modeling capabilities
avoid_when: Simple LP/MIP problems — PuLP or OR-Tools simpler
When Google OR-Tools' CP-SAT solver coverage is sufficient
summary: Pyomo is Python's most powerful optimization modeling language — solver-agnostic LP/MIP/NLP. ConcreteModel() with Var, Objective, Constraint objects. SolverFactory('glpk').solve(model). Supports: GLPK, CBC (free), Gurobi/CPLEX (commercial), IPOPT (nonlinear). Abstract and Concrete models, stochastic programming. More expressive than PuLP or OR-Tools but steeper learning curve. Best for complex optimization problems requiring advanced modeling (nonlinear, stochastic, multi-stage).
